nodes.label:
Research
Funding
nodes.title: Government grants, corporate R&D budgets, and venture capital flowing into quantum computing research programs.

nodes.label:
Published
Results
nodes.title: Peer-reviewed papers, preprints, and technical reports claiming progress toward quantum advantage or error correction milestones.

nodes.label:
Media
Coverage
nodes.title: Journalists amplify quantum announcements, often without technical scrutiny, framing incremental progress as imminent breakthroughs.

nodes.label:
Public
Expectations
nodes.title: Widespread belief that quantum computers will soon revolutionize cryptography, drug discovery, and optimization — often far exceeding what the physics supports.

nodes.label:
Political
Pressure
nodes.title: Elected officials and policymakers push for quantum funding to avoid perceived technology gaps with rival nations.

nodes.label:
Industry
Investment
nodes.title: Private-sector capital from tech giants and startups, driven by FOMO and media narratives rather than demonstrated commercial viability.

nodes.label:
Actual
Progress
nodes.title: Real advances in qubit coherence, gate fidelity, and error rates — typically incremental and far short of what commercial applications require.

nodes.label:
Hype
Gap
nodes.title: The growing divergence between public expectations of quantum computing and what has actually been demonstrated. This gap represents systemic risk.

edges.title: Enables: Research funding produces published results, regardless of whether they represent genuine breakthroughs.
Generates: Published results become raw material for media stories, often stripped of caveats and limitations.
Inflates: Uncritical media coverage raises public expectations far beyond what the actual results warrant.
Drives: High public expectations create political pressure on officials to increase quantum funding.
Increases: Political pressure translates directly into larger research budgets and grant programs.
Funds: Industry investment provides resources for hardware development and experimental progress.
Produces: Actual progress generates publishable results, though often overstated in significance.
Attracts: Positive media coverage draws industry investment through FOMO and competitive pressure.
Reveals: Actual progress exposes how far reality lags behind expectations, widening the hype gap from the reality side.
Widens: Rising public expectations push the hype gap wider from the expectations side.
Dampens: As the hype gap becomes undeniable, savvy investors pull back, reducing industry investment.

R1.label: R1: Hype Cycle
R1.description: The public hype reinforcing loop: Funding produces results, media amplifies them, public expectations rise, political pressure increases, and more funding flows in.

R2.label: R2: Investment Cycle
R2.description: The industry investment reinforcing loop: Investment funds progress, progress produces results, media covers results, coverage attracts more investment.

B1.label: B1: Reality Check
B1.description: The balancing loop: Actual progress reveals the gap between expectations and reality, which should dampen investment — but the reinforcing loops often overpower it.
